name: French Country Style Potatoes
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup half and half, milk or heavy cream
recipeInstructions:
1. Scrub the potatoes without peeling them. Cut into 1/4-inch (1/2 cm) slices – about 6 cups total. Do not rinse the sliced potatoes; the starch will help thicken the mixture.
2. Place the potatoes, water, and chicken stock in a 3-quart (3 l) saucepan. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes or until partially cooked, stirring occasionally.
3. Add half-and-half, garlic, salt, and pepper. Increase heat to medium-high. Bring to a boil and boil for 2 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent scorching. Remove from heat.
4. In an oven-safe dish, spread 1 tablespoon of butter or margarine on the bottom. Layer half of the mushrooms, half of the potatoes with half of the sauce, and drizzle with remaining 2 tablespoons butter or margarine.
5. Repeat layering: half of the mushrooms, half of the potatoes with the remaining sauce, and drizzle with any leftover butter or margarine.
6. Top with remaining mushrooms and sauce. Sprinkle with parsley.
7. Cover with foil and bake in a preheated 350°F oven for 30 minutes. Uncover and bake an additional 20 minutes longer or until the potatoes are tender when pierced with a fork.
8. Serves 6 - 8.
